TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Giɫgiɫaw of Vaɫulu village

S.FORM: aitsu a ɫequ nu qezemezemetj a sipangtjengtjezan a paɫequ.
S.PHON: aiʦu a ʎəqu nu qəzəməzəməc a sipaŋcəŋcəzan a paʎəqu.
S.FORM: aicu a ljequ nu qezemezemetj a sipangtjengtjezan a paljequ.
S.PHON: aiʦu a ʎəqu nu qəzəməzəməʦ a sipaŋʦəŋʦəzan a paʎəqu.
S.TRANSL: It is at night that owls come and hoot.

S.FORM: anema tja keɫang tuki anema sipangtjengtjez nua ɫequ nu qezemezemetj a paɫequ.
S.PHON: anəma ca kəʎaŋ tuki anəma sipaŋcəŋcəz nua ʎəqu nu qəzəməzəməc a paʎəqu.
S.FORM: anema tja keljang tuki anema sipangtjengtjez nua ljequ nu qezemezemetj a paljequ.
S.PHON: anəma ʦa kəʎaŋ tuki anəma sipaŋʦəŋʦəz nua ʎəqu nu qəzəməzəməʦ a paʎəqu.
S.TRANSL: We don’t know the reason why owls come and hoot at night.

S.FORM: ɫakua uri keman tua kakeḍian mana sipangtjengtjez, tja ayain anga a mareka kakeḍian a benuru.
S.PHON: ʎakua uri kəman tua kakəɖian mana sipaŋcəŋcəz, ca ajain aŋa a marəka kakəɖian a bənuru.
S.FORM: ljakua uri keman tua kakedrian mana sipangtjengtjez, tja ayain anga a mareka kakedrian a benuru.
S.PHON: ʎakua uri kəman tua kakəɖian mana sipaŋʦəŋʦəz, ʦa ajain aŋa a marəka kakəɖian a bənuru.
S.TRANSL: But we lie to our children and tell them they come to eat children.

S.FORM: aitsu nu qemauqaung, nu tsemulitsuli, nu ika kilangda nu tja qaqivuin a mareka tja aɫak, "ku sidjauɫan tua ɫequ, uɫa sun a kani," nu tja ayain, patjezuanga tua qemaung tua maɫekuya.
S.PHON: aiʦu nu qəmauqauŋ, nu ʦəmuɭiʦuɭi, nu ika kiɭaŋda nu ca qaqivuin a marəka ca aʎak, "ku siɉauʎan tua ʎəqu, uʎa sun a kani," nu ca ajain, pacəzuaŋa tua qəmauŋ tua maʎəkuja.
S.FORM: aicu nu qemauqaung, nu cemuliculi, nu ika kilangda nu tja qaqivuin a mareka tja aljak, "ku sidjauljan tua ljequ, ulja sun a kani," nu tja ayain, patjezuanga tua qemaung tua maljekuya.
S.PHON: aiʦu nu qəmauqauŋ, nu ʦəmuɭiʦuɭi, nu ika kiɭaŋda nu ʦa qaqivuin a marəka ʦa aʎak, "ku siɉauʎan tua ʎəqu, uʎa sun a kani," nu ʦa ajain, paʦəzuaŋa tua qəmauŋ tua maʎəkuja.
S.TRANSL: When our children cry or won’t eat or don’t do what we say, if we say: “I’ll call the owl to eat you up”, then they stop crying or being bad.

S.FORM: a ɫequ ika uri kemɫakeɫang tuki karekurekutji timadju.
S.PHON: a ʎəqu ika uri kəmʎakəʎaŋ tuki karəkurəkuci timaɉu.
S.FORM: a ljequ ika uri kemljakeljang tuki karekurekutji timadju.
S.PHON: a ʎəqu ika uri kəmʎakəʎaŋ tuki karəkurəkuʦi timaɉu.
S.TRANSL: The owl won’t know that he is used to frighten children;

S.FORM: qau saka kakarekutjan nua kakeḍian timadju.
S.PHON: qau saka kakarəkucan nua kakəɖian timaɉu.
S.FORM: qau saka kakarekutjan nua kakedrian timadju.
S.PHON: qau saka kakarəkuʦan nua kakəɖian timaɉu.
S.TRANSL: but he still is a bogey for children.

S.FORM: a kakeḍian uta ika uri kemɫakeɫang tuki qayaqayam a ika uri kemuda a karekurekutjan niamadju.
S.PHON: a kakəɖian uta ika uri kəmʎakəʎaŋ tuki qajaqajam a ika uri kəmuda a karəkurəkucan niamaɉu.
S.FORM: a kakedrian uta ika uri kemljakeljang tuki qayaqayam a ika uri kemuda a karekurekutjan niamadju.
S.PHON: a kakəɖian uta ika uri kəmʎakəʎaŋ tuki qajaqajam a ika uri kəmuda a karəkurəkuʦan niamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: And the children aren’t to know that what they are frightened of is a bird that won’t do anything to them.
